title: requests — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by `AccessRequestServiceProvider::boot()` so any FormRequest / Data class can validate the shape of an access-request submission. Applied to `CreateAccessRequestData` at the write path.

rules.name: valid_requestable_resource
rules.description: Ensures the `resource_type` string is present in AccessRequestableRegistry. Rejects at write time if the caller submits a resource_type not registered by any consumer module. Fires ACCESS_REQUEST_INVALID_REQUESTABLE_RESOURCE (422).
rules.default_message: The resource type :attribute is not available for access requests.

rules.name: resource_in_caller_tenant
rules.description: Ensures the row identified by (`resource_type_field`, `resource_id`) exists in the caller's active tenant. The rule resolves the target model class from the registry, queries by primary key + tenant scope, and refuses if the row is not found. Fires ACCESS_REQUEST_RESOURCE_NOT_IN_CALLER_TENANT (403) OR ACCESS_REQUEST_RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND (404).

params.name: resource_type_field
params.description: The name of the sibling form field carrying the resource_type value (defaults to 'resource_type').

rules.default_message: The resource :attribute is not accessible in your current tenant.

rules.name: permission_belongs_to_resource_type
rules.description: Ensures every entry in the `permissions_wanted` array is (a) declared on the target resource type via `#[AccessRequestable(permissions: [...])]` AND (b) a valid permission name on the sanctum guard for the caller's application. Rule iterates the array and rejects the WHOLE request on the first failure. Fires ACCESS_REQUEST_PERMISSION_NOT_ON_RESOURCE_TYPE (422).

params.name: resource_type_field
params.description: Sibling field carrying the resource_type value.

rules.default_message: The permission :permission is not requestable on :resource_type.

rules.name: valid_reason_length
rules.description: Ensures the `reason` field is between MIN (default 20) and MAX (default 1000) characters after trimming. Short reasons don't inform approvers; over-long reasons are usually copy-paste artefacts. Multibyte-safe (mb_strlen).
rules.default_message: The reason must be between :min and :max characters (:length given).

compositionOrder: Every CreateAccessRequestData field-rule composition MUST list `valid_requestable_resource` on resource_type BEFORE `resource_in_caller_tenant` and BEFORE `permission_belongs_to_resource_type` — the latter two depend on the registry lookup succeeding.

Rule composition: `resource_type: [required, string, valid_requestable_resource]`. `resource_id: [required, string, resource_in_caller_tenant:resource_type]`. `permissions_wanted: [required, array, min:1, permission_belongs_to_resource_type:resource_type]`. `reason: [required, string, valid_reason_length:20,1000]`.
